article_title: Godot 4.7 Ships With Desktop HDR Output, an AreaLight3D Node, and a Rebuilt Asset Store
summary: Accurate, neutral report on the Godot 4.7 release; every quote, number, name, and date verified verbatim against the cited source snapshots.

content_preview.title: Godot 4.7 Ships With Desktop HDR Output, an AreaLight3D Node, and a Rebuilt Asset Store
content_preview.summary: The open-source game engine's June feature release adds HDR output across five platforms, rectangular area lights, wasm64 web exports, and a new Asset Store.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Godot, the free and open-source cross-platform game engine, released version 4.7 as the `4.7-stable` tag on June 18, 2026, according to the engine's [GitHub releases page](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/releases). The release, nicknamed "Lights, Camera, Action!", leads with high dynamic range output for desktop and several other platforms, a new rectangular light node, and a rebuilt asset marketplace, per the [official release page](https://godotengine.org/releases/4.7/).

God...

recommendations: Consider adding godotengine.org and xda-developers.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as recognized primary/secondary sources.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (567 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Sections map cleanly to the release's headline features (HDR, AreaLight3D, Asset Store, tooling, new platforms, scale), with an honest 'What We Don't Know' section flagging the absence of benchmarks and a firm Steam Frame ship date.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-1 (github.com/godotengine/godot/releases): confirmed the '4.7-stable' tag dated '18 Jun' with asset timestamp 2026-06-18T12:06:18Z, and the 'feature release improving upon... preserve compatibility' quote verbatim (Rule 9 GitHub release-tag date satisfied). source-0 (godotengine.org/releases/4.7/): verified 'Lights, Camera, Action!', the HDR-output quote and platform list, 'real-time light from a rectangle in 3D space', VirtualJoystick '3 operation modes', 'production-ready for the Steam Frame, planned to release this summer', and the contributor/PR counts ('Well over 300 contributors... totalling over 1,600 pull requests') verbatim — the counts are cited to the source that carries them. source-3 (beta-1 dev snapshot): 'posterchild for 4.7 overall', '309 contributors submitted 1265 fixes since the release of 4.6-stable', and 'wasm64 support on web builds' verbatim. source-2 (GamingOnLinux): 'brand new Asset Store has launched', DrawableTexture2D, 'Vulkan subsampled images', 'Steam Frame and Android XR support', picture-in-picture, and 'Initial touch support for Wayland on Linux' verbatim. source-4 (XDA): 'not X11', 'internally rendered lighting in HDR for a long time, but the full detail can't be seen', the AreaLight3D description, and 'TV screens, billboards' verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (release date, version tag, platform list, node names, operation modes, contributor/PR counts) traces to a cited source. No hallucinated quotes or misattributions found; counts attributed to beta-1 vs final-release pages are kept distinct and correct.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Attributive framing throughout ('per the official release page', 'XDA Developers reports'); no sensationalism, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Godot 4.7 coverage in the archive; the two earlier Godot mentions are unrelated (open-source AI slop, GDC neural-rendering convergence).
concerns: The automated check flagged godotengine.org and xda-developers.com as not in the source allowlist. godotengine.org is the project's official primary source (canonical for the release and acceptable under Rule 9 alongside the GitHub release tag), and xda-developers.com is an established technology outlet. Both are used appropriately; this is a curation gap in the allowlist, not a content defect, so it does not warrant a corrections record.
recommendations: Add godotengine.org and xda-developers.com to config/source_allowlist.txt to suppress this warning on future game-engine and consumer-tech coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-sourced release report with no factual or attribution errors. Approving as-is; the sole automated finding is an allowlist curation note, not a publishable correction.
